title: memo-init Anchor-Term Register Schema
description: Org register of anchor terms — a policy-block adjunct per meta-spec 06-conventions-writing.md, NOT a new registry primitive. Mechanics from OASF (stable id, SemVer, known mis-labels); entry fields from SKOS (canonical label, definition, negative delimitation). No URIs, no triples, no reasoner.

policyBlock.description: The kind:policy block this register registers under — the register is an adjunct of that block, never a parallel machine.

id.description: Stable identifier (OASF) — outlives a re-labelling, never reused, never renumbered.

label.description: Canonical preferred label (SKOS prefLabel) — exactly one per term (AT1).

definition.description: In-document definition (SKOS definition), owned by exactly one chapter (AT2).

negativeDelimitation.description: What the term is NOT (SKOS scopeNote, sharpened) — the neighbouring meaning a reader's prior would supply (AT3). Required and non-empty: a term without a negative delimitation is not an anchor term.

misLabels.description: Known wrong wordings — a warning signal for the usage lint (OASF). Empty array means 'none known', stated explicitly.

version.description: SemVer of the entry — a meaning change is a version bump; the id stays fixed.

owningFamily.description: The family whose chapter owns the in-document definition (AT2).

owningChapter.description: The chapter stem carrying the definition; the full path is resolved via the family head, never hardcoded.

namespaceToken.description: Optional cross-family qualifier for the same label used in different namespaces.
